title: Brown sugar vanilla lip scrub
subtitle: A gentle exfoliating lip scrub made with brown sugar, sweet almond oil, and vanilla
excerpt: A brown sugar lip scrub removes dry, flaking skin from the lips by mechanical exfoliation. Sweet almond oil moisturises while the sugar granules work. Vanilla extract and a little honey make the blend palatable; a small amount is applied, rubbed gently for 30 seconds, then rinsed or licked off.
sourceNotes: Mechanical lip exfoliation with sugar scrubs: standard cosmetic formulation reference. Brown sugar particle size as gentle exfoliant: Draelos, Cosmetic Dermatology (2010). All ingredients in this formula are food-grade and incidental ingestion is not a safety concern.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. A 25 g pot provides around 10 to 15 uses.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Stable for 3 months at room temperature. The sugar acts as a natural humectant and preservative in high concentration.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Three months. The sugar concentration in a properly made scrub inhibits microbial growth naturally, but moisture introduced by wet fingers will dilute this. Use a small spatula rather than dipping fingers. Discard if the scrub smells off or shows visible growth.

glossaryTerms.definition: A water-free preparation. This scrub contains no water, so it does not need a chemical preservative; the sugar itself acts as a natural preservative.

content.text: A lip scrub is the simplest category of cosmetic preparation: an abrasive in an oil or fat base, applied to the lips, worked gently, then removed. This

blend uses brown sugar for its softer, rounder granules compared to white sugar, sweet almond oil for conditioning, and honey for texture and a slightly sticky quality that helps the scrub stay on the lips. Vanilla extract adds flavour and a pleasant scent. All ingredients are food-safe; some will inevitably be ingested.

items.prepNote: 60% of scrub; provides the abrasive action
28% of scrub; or coconut oil, which has a pleasant taste
8% of scrub; for texture and humectant properties

content.text: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl and stir until evenly mixed. The mixture should have the texture of a coarse paste. Transfer to a small tin or pot with a lid. No heat is needed.

Apply a small amount to dry lips using a clean fingertip or spatula. Rub in gentle circles for 20 to 30 seconds, then rinse with warm water or wipe with a damp cloth. Follow with a lip balm. Use once or twice a week; daily use on already soft lips provides no additional benefit.

Keep in a cool place with the lid on. Use a clean spatula rather than wet fingers; introducing moisture dilutes the sugar concentration and shortens the shelf life. Keeps three months. Discard if the scrub smells off or changes colour significantly.
